commit.message:
fix: miniapp slot assignments now include from/to for schedule engine

The schedule engine (scheduleEngineV0) matches SHIFT_ASSIGNMENT facts by
dow|from|to key, but the miniapp was creating facts with only slot_name
(e.g. "Утро") without from/to times. This caused all miniapp-created
assignments to be silently ignored by the engine.

Now the miniapp slot update endpoint:
- Reads shift times from settings (shifts.morning.from/to etc)
- Includes from, to, date, assigned_user_id in the fact payload
- Deletes old facts matching either slot_name OR from/to format

repository.name: asupersync
repository.description: Async runtime for Rust where correctness is structural: region-owned tasks, cancel-correct protocols, capability-gated effects, and deterministic replay testing

commit.message:
fix(time): move waker registration into TimeoutFuture::poll_with_time

poll_with_time() checked the inner future and the deadline but did not
register the task waker with the sleep future, meaning the caller had
to follow up with a redundant Pin::new(&mut timeout).poll(cx) to
ensure the reactor would wake the task when the deadline expired.
Both happy_eyeballs and tcp/stream had this two-step pattern.

This commit adds `let _ = Pin::new(&mut self.sleep).poll(cx)` at the
end of poll_with_time's Pending path, making waker registration
self-contained within the method. The two call sites in
happy_eyeballs.rs and tcp/stream.rs are simplified from the awkward
poll_with_time-then-poll dance to a single poll_with_time call inside
poll_fn.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test(runtime): add worker idle retirement test with custom time getter

New test worker_idle_retirement_uses_custom_time_getter verifies that
the blocking pool's idle worker retirement path correctly consults the
custom time_getter hook when deciding whether an idle worker has
exceeded its timeout.

The test creates a pool with min_threads=0, max_threads=1, a 5ms idle
timeout, and the existing stepped_timeout_time as the time source.
It spawns one task, waits for completion, then uses a Condvar to wait
(up to 1s wall time) for the on_thread_stop callback to fire — proving
the synthetic clock drove retirement without requiring real wall-clock
sleeps.

Assertions verify: the worker was retired (callback fired), active
thread count dropped to 0, and the scripted clock was consulted at
least twice (entry into idle loop + subsequent check).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(test): make GracePeriodGuard test time source thread-local

The grace period tests used a global AtomicU64 + OnceLock<Instant>
for the synthetic clock, meaning concurrent test threads could
interfere with each other's time state. This is particularly
problematic under cargo test's default parallel execution.

Switched both TEST_GRACE_TIME_BASE and TEST_GRACE_TIME_NANOS to
thread_local! storage:
- TEST_GRACE_TIME_BASE: thread_local Instant (each thread gets its own base)
- TEST_GRACE_TIME_NANOS: thread_local Cell<u64> (no atomic needed)
- test_grace_time_now(): reads both from thread-local context
- All test sites: .store() → .with(|n| n.set()), .load() → .with(Cell::get)

Removed unused imports: OnceLock, AtomicU64, Ordering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: asupersync
repository.description: Async runtime for Rust where correctness is structural: region-owned tasks, cancel-correct protocols, capability-gated effects, and deterministic replay testing

commit.message:
fix(service): remove Unpin requirement from RateLimit and add NotReady to Reconnect

Two service layer improvements:

RateLimit (rate_limit.rs):
- RateLimitFuture was an enum that used get_mut() + Pin::new() to
  poll the inner future, requiring F: Unpin. This prevented wrapping
  services whose futures are !Unpin (the common case for async fn).
- Refactored to use pin_project: RateLimitFuture is now a struct
  with a #[pin] state field, and RateLimitFutureState is the inner
  enum with #[pin] on the Inner variant's future field.
- The Service impl for RateLimit<S> drops the S::Future: Unpin bound.
- Future::poll uses self.project().state.project() for safe pin
  projection through both layers.
- Error variant's take() fallback changed from panic to
  RateLimitExceeded (more graceful on double-poll).
- load_shed.rs: import reorder (Arc before AtomicBool).

Reconnect (reconnect.rs):
- Added ReconnectError::NotReady variant for when call() is invoked
  without a preceding poll_ready() (or after disconnect). Previously
  this path hit expect("poll_ready must be called before call") which
  panics — a bad failure mode for a service combinator.
- ReconnectFuture refactored from a flat struct to a state machine
  (Inner / Error / Done variants) so it can return NotReady errors
  without needing an actual inner future.
- The Future impl takes self by get_mut() + mem::replace (the inner
  future is still Unpin-bounded via the Service impl bounds, so this
  is safe) to avoid borrow issues with the multi-field state.
- New Service bounds add E: Unpin and M::Error: Unpin for the state
  machine transitions.
- New tests: lazy_call_without_poll_ready_returns_not_ready,
  call_after_disconnect_returns_not_ready, reconnect_error_not_ready_display
- Display impl for NotReady, source() returns None for NotReady.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor(server): capture timer driver at ShutdownSignal construction

ShutdownSignal previously called Cx::current() on every current_time()
invocation to find a timer driver, meaning the time source could
silently change if the Cx was swapped or cleared between calls. This
also meant the signal had no way to remember which driver it was
supposed to use after construction.

This commit introduces ShutdownTimeSource, a three-variant enum:
- WallClock: uses crate::time::wall_now() (default when no Cx)
- TimerDriver(handle): clones the TimerDriverHandle from the Cx at
  construction time, freezing the time source for the signal's lifetime
- Custom(fn): for with_time_getter() callers (test injection)

ShutdownSignal::new() now calls capture_from_current() which snapshots
the timer driver from the current Cx (if any) into a TimerDriver
variant, or falls back to WallClock. The handle is Clone + Send + Sync
so the signal remains portable across threads.

Also moves the sleep-until-with-time-getter logic from connection.rs
into ShutdownSignal::wait_until(), since the signal now owns the time
source and can drive the sleep directly. ConnectionManager::wait_for_close
calls self.shutdown_signal.wait_until(deadline) instead of the removed
free function.

Removed:
- default_time_getter() free function
- sleep_until_with_time_getter() from connection.rs
- time_getter() accessor (callers use current_time() directly)
- Unused imports from connection.rs (poll_fn, Pin, Poll, sleep_until)

New test:
- new_captures_timer_driver_from_current_context: constructs a Cx with
  a VirtualClock at 42s, creates a ShutdownSignal inside that Cx scope,
  verifies drain_start/deadline use the captured driver, then advances
  the virtual clock and checks that collect_stats reflects the advance

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: asupersync
repository.description: Async runtime for Rust where correctness is structural: region-owned tasks, cancel-correct protocols, capability-gated effects, and deterministic replay testing

commit.message:
refactor(net): decouple DNS resolver from ambient Cx context

The Resolver had two implicit dependencies on the current Cx:

1. default_timeout_now() probed Cx::current() to find a timer driver
   for deadline calculation, making DNS timeout behavior depend on
   whether a Cx happened to be set on the current thread.

2. spawn_blocking_dns() checked Cx::current() for a blocking pool
   handle, routing DNS work through the runtime's pool when available
   and falling back to a dedicated thread otherwise — causing
   inconsistent thread identity depending on ambient context.

Both are removed. The resolver now always uses wall_now() for timeout
deadlines (via the existing time_getter field) and always routes
synchronous DNS/connect work through spawn_blocking_on_thread()
regardless of ambient context.

Changes:
- Remove default_timeout_now() helper entirely
- Resolver::new(): set time_getter to crate::time::wall_now directly
- spawn_blocking_dns(): remove Cx::current() check, always use
  spawn_blocking_on_thread() with a comment explaining the design
- Remove unused imports: Cx, spawn_blocking module
- Add Cx import to test module (only needed there now)

New tests:
- resolver_default_timeout_deadline_ignores_current_cx_timer_driver:
  sets up a Cx with a virtual clock at 5s, creates a Resolver, and
  verifies the timeout deadline follows wall clock, not the virtual
  timer driver
- resolver_blocking_dns_ignores_current_pool_and_uses_dedicated_thread:
  sets up a Cx with a blocking pool handle, runs spawn_blocking_dns,
  and verifies the work runs on "asupersync-blocking" thread, not
  the pool

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(net): stop accumulating stale interest flags on reactor re-registration

The register_interest methods in UdpSocket, UnixDatagram, UnixStream,
and process pipe I/O all used `registration.interest() | interest` to
combine the previously registered interest with the newly requested
one before calling rearm(). This meant interest flags were monotonically
accumulated but never cleared — once a socket registered for both Read
and Write, it could never drop back to Read-only even if only reads
were pending, causing unnecessary wakeups when the write side became
ready with no writer waiting.

This commit removes the interest accumulation pattern at all four
sites: each rearm() call now passes exactly the interest requested by
the current poll operation. The reactor's edge-triggered design means
stale interest is harmless for correctness (spurious wakeups just
re-poll), but removing accumulation reduces unnecessary wake traffic
on busy sockets.

Affected files:
- src/net/udp.rs: remove mut binding and bitwise-OR of old interest
- src/net/unix/datagram.rs: use interest directly instead of combined
- src/net/unix/stream.rs: same pattern
- src/process.rs: same pattern
- src/net/tcp/listener.rs: formatting-only cleanup (line wrapping)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(cx): migrate checkpoint timestamps from wall-clock Instant to runtime Time

CheckpointState tracked progress using std::time::Instant, which
means the deadline monitor compared wall-clock durations against
logical runtime deadlines — a category mismatch that caused false
stale-progress warnings in deterministic/lab runtimes where wall
time barely advances while logical time races ahead, and missed
warnings in the inverse scenario.

This commit switches CheckpointState::last_checkpoint from
Option<Instant> to Option<Time> (the runtime's logical clock type):

task_context.rs:
- last_checkpoint field: Instant → Time
- record() / record_at(): accept Time instead of Instant
- record_with_message() / record_with_message_at(): same migration
- Fallback record()/record_with_message() now call crate::time::wall_now()
  instead of Instant::now(), keeping standalone (non-runtime) usage working
- Tests updated to use Time::from_nanos() literals instead of Instant::now()

cx.rs:
- New helper current_checkpoint_time(): reads the timer driver handle
  from Cx's handles (if present) to get the authoritative runtime
  clock, falling back to wall_clock_now() when no driver exists
- checkpoint() and checkpoint_with(): capture current_checkpoint_time()
  before acquiring the inner write lock, then pass it to record_at()
  / record_with_message_at() — this ensures the timestamp is taken at
  the call site rather than after potentially contended lock acquisition
- checkpoint_state() doc example simplified to just check .is_some()
  since the Time type doesn't have an .elapsed() method like Instant

deadline_monitor.rs:
- MonitoredTask: last_progress renamed to last_progress_wall (still
  Instant, tracks wall-clock for checkpoint_timeout comparison);
  last_checkpoint_seen changed from Option<Instant> to Option<Time>;
  new field last_checkpoint_count_seen: u64 for count-based freshness
- New task registration: last_progress_time uses the checkpoint's
  runtime Time (or task.created_at() as fallback) instead of the
  current scan instant
- Existing task update: uses checkpoint_count > entry count (instead
  of checkpoint timestamp ordering) to detect fresh checkpoints,
  avoiding false negatives when two checkpoints occur at the same
  logical time
- checkpoint_interval calculation: uses Time::duration_since (returns
  nanos) wrapped in Duration::from_nanos
- Wall-no-progress check: uses saturating_duration_since to prevent
  panic if the Instant source is non-monotonic (e.g. mocked clocks)
- Scan interval gate: also uses saturating_duration_since for safety
- Tests: all make_task calls updated to pass Option<Time> instead of
  Option<Instant>; removed test_now_minus helper (no longer needed);
  three new tests: repeated_checkpoint_count_resets_progress,
  no_warning_when_checkpoint_time_ahead_of_monitor_clock,
  check_interval_tolerates_time_getter_going_backwards

audit/ambient.rs:
- AMBIENT_VIOLATION_CEILING lowered from 120 to 118, reflecting the
  two removed ambient Cx::current() calls in dns/resolver.rs and
  server/shutdown.rs (committed separately but counted here)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: revert is_admin default to true, fix L2/L3 incident filter, eliminate transaction poisoning

Three fixes:
1. Migration 082: revert app.is_admin default to 'true' — SQLAlchemy
   sessions (admin dashboard) don't call SET LOCAL, so fail-closed broke
   all admin queries. RLS enforcement is at the application layer via
   tenant_connection(), not the database default.

2. admin_connection() no longer wraps in conn.transaction() — eliminates
   transaction poisoning for multi-query endpoints (checkins, fleet overview).
   Relies on database default is_admin='true'. tenant_connection() still
   wraps in transaction for SET LOCAL scoping.

3. Wire level filter into incidents endpoint — the L1/L2/L3 filter buttons
   were accepted but never passed to get_incidents_from_db().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: eva_flags on TB lines — tag/untag when findings created/addressed

1. Add eva_flags JSONField (default=list) to TrialBalanceLine storing
   check_name strings of EvaFindings that flagged each row.

2. Rewrite eva_finding_resolve to run inside transaction.atomic():
   (a) set finding ADDRESSED + resolution metadata,
   (b) create ActivityLog with EVA_FINDING_ADDRESSED event type,
       severity in description, full resolution_note, and finding FK,
   (c) remove check_name from eva_flags on all tagged TB lines via
       untag_tb_lines_for_finding() + bulk_update.

3. Add tag_tb_lines_with_finding() helper called after every
   EvaFinding.save() in the Eva review task — both the primary LLM
   path (uses affected_account_codes from LLM JSON) and the risk
   engine fallback path (uses RiskFlag.affected_accounts).

4. Add affected_account_codes field to the LLM JSON output schema
   so Eva returns the specific account codes each finding relates to.

commit.message:
Add pasted events block to Events channel

Editors can paste pre-formatted events from an external service into the
newsletter. Stored on newsletter_instances (not as a module) so it's easy
to remove when API integration is built. Includes inline TipTap editing,
before/after position toggle, debounced auto-save, undo support, and
preview rendering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: initialize @resq/ui — 55 shadcn components + Storybook

- 55 components migrated into per-component folders (src/components/<name>/)
- src/hooks/use-mobile.ts, src/lib/utils.ts (cn helper)
- src/styles/globals.css with Tailwind v4 CSS variable theme
- Storybook 8 with @storybook/nextjs: Button and Card stories
- tsdown unbundle build → lib/ with per-file tree-shaking
- tsconfig updated for react-jsx and DOM libs
- 58-entry exports map (root + 55 components + styles/globals.css + lib/utils)
- peerDeps: react>=19, react-dom>=19, tailwindcss>=4
- publishConfig: access public, provenance true

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Merge Pin into DJ Lock, unify lock styling, reduce sync to 5s

- Single "DJ Lock" menu item replaces separate Lock + Pin buttons
- Lock API accepts optional position param (merges pin functionality)
- All locked songs use yellow styling (removed blue pin styling)
- Votes hidden for all locked songs on both guest and dashboard views
- Sync interval reduced from 10s to 5s for snappier transitions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
